summary: RF-04 satisfies scope and Verify Steps: offline replay is reproducible, trust boundaries, provenance, immutable anchor, counts, and fail-closed behavior are confirmed with no P0/P1/P2 findings.
findings: 50/50 runs and 55/55 provider episodes link to 50 canonical envelopes and 50 content-addressed evidence bundles; 70/70 outcomes, 27/27 provider-token cells, and 170/170 scalar cells resolve exactly.
CURRENT_AGENT maps to CODER only for immutable-anchor lifecycle actors while prompts, episode ledger, role usage, and telemetry retain CURRENT_AGENT.
JSONL parsing remains fail-closed: one turn.completed, required provider usage, last valid pre-completion status, no post-completion status, and bounded output channels.
Post-main rebase preserved the exact RF-04 patch by range-diff and patch SHA256; historical baseline blob remains cc5eb8600d41b7da3adf19ee06b58227caaab064.
evidence_refs: Focused Vitest: 4 files, 40/40 passed; offline checker structural 006ddc6d and diagnostics 01c7b818; full ci:contract passed
hidden_assumptions: Provider token counts are authoritative from the pinned Codex turn.completed event and are not independently reconciled with billing.
Five runs per scenario under one fixed profile define a control baseline, not a statistically general performance benchmark.
CURRENT_AGENT to CODER is valid only for this immutable anchor and must be revalidated for another anchor.
residual_risks: Capture is coupled to pinned Codex CLI/model/reasoning/platform identities; version and hash drift fail closed.
Latency and fs.watch diagnostics remain platform-noisy and are separated from structural comparison.
The rewritten PR head still requires guarded publication and fresh hosted checks before integration.
